risks.description: High carbon taxes on steel and iron will increase material costs for wind turbines, raising overall turbine expenses.
Failure to meet Scope 1/2/3 GHG emission targets (e.g., decarbonizing steel suppliers) could lead to financial penalties or reputational damage.
Slow adoption of low-carbon alternatives (e.g., steel) may force Vestas to absorb higher costs, impacting profitability and competitiveness.
Unforeseen political shifts (e.g., carbon tariffs) could disrupt market stability and increase operational costs.
Increased energy use in manufacturing/construction may strain operations and emissions, despite industry growth.
Reliance on fossil fuels (e.g., Scope 1) and grid electricity (Scope 2) exposes Vestas to rising emission costs.
Rapid expansion of wind energy may create supply chain bottlenecks or price fluctuations in components.
Increased use of sustainability criteria in tenders could exclude Vestas if emissions targets aren’t met.
mitigation: Enabling businesses/society to reduce GHG emissions

risks.description: Over-reliance on fossil fuels for operations may lead to supply shortages and higher costs.
